sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a quadruped (which chews the cud like a hare), Levit. 11:5 Deu 14:7 which lives gregariously on rocks, and is remarked for its cunning, Psa 104:18 Pro 30:26. The Rabbins render it coney; more correctly the LXX. in three places, χοιρογρύλλιος, i.e. mus jaculus Linn., Arab. يربوع, Jerboa, an animal of the size of the coney, with a head resembling that of a hog, with long hind legs fitted for leaping; it inhabits burrows dug in the mud, and is remarkably cunning. It is either so called from its burrows in which it hides itself, or from its cunning. See Bochart, Hieroz. i. p. 1001, seqq. Oedmann, Verm. Sammlungen, iv. p. 48.

(2) [Shaphan], pr.n.-(a) a scribe of Josiah, 2Ki 22:3, 12 2Ki 22:12 Jer 36:10, compare Eze 8:11.-(b) 2Ki 22:12 25:22 Jer 26:24 39:14 .
